hook.text: A niche is not where a species lives—it's how that species makes a living.

buildup.text: It includes what a species eats, when it's active, and how it avoids predators.

discovery.text: No two species can hold the exact same niche in the same place indefinitely.

twist.text: Darwin's finches evolved different beaks to fill different food niches on one archipelago.

climax.text: Niche partitioning explains why tropical forests host thousands of species side by side.

quiz.question: What is an ecological niche?
options: How a species makes its living
The physical location where a species lives
The size of a species' population
